ior System Engineer (Project: CPI) Full-time - On-site

Ibrahim Abdel Aziz is a Cloud Operations Architect (DevOps) with contact information including email, phone number, GitHub, LinkedIn, and personal website.

Ibrahim Abdel Aziz completed a Post Graduate Diploma in Information Technology from Symbiosis Institute in January 2013 in Pune, Maharashtra, India, and a Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics and Telecommunications from Pune University in January 2007, also in Pune, Maharashtra, India.

From February 2007 to January 2009, the individual worked as a Senior System Engineer at Patni Computer Systems Limited in Alexandria, Egypt, focusing on the CPI Maintenance Project for Genworth Imaging Solutions, providing L1 support for the P8 FileNet application, and managing user tickets.

The individual administered 10 Linux Proxy servers and MySQL servers, implementing MySQL replication, and developed SQL queries for client users, managing Oracle databases and deploying queries to production.

The individual ensured optimal functionality of applications like eProcess, Trexo, and customized FileNet instances, and wrote shell scripts to automate tasks, including deleting logs older than five days on production servers.

From May 2013 to the present, the individual developed shell scripts to automate AWS cloud operations, including identifying and shutting down idle instances and reclaiming unused Elastic IP addresses, and implemented AWS Lambda functions for event-driven automation.

From May 2013 to the present, the individual recruited and built a 15-member team in India, leading 4 concurrent projects focused on Tibco Spotfire enterprise and cloud product support, and managed deployment coordination and hotfixes.
From February 2007 to April 2013, the individual implemented shell scripting for Linux administration, managed MySQL database replication, and provided support for customized applications.

The individual possesses skills in Cloud Platforms (AWS, Azure, etc.), DevOps & Automation (Shell Scripting, Python, etc.), Databases (MySQL, Oracle, SQL), Operating Systems (Linux/Unix, IBM AIX, Windows), Tools & Technologies (Tibco Spotfire, P8 FileNet, etc.), and Methodologies (DevOps, Solution Architecture).

From May 2013 to the present, Ibrahim Abdel Aziz worked as a Cloud Operations Architect (DevOps) at Egy-Tech Solutions in Cairo, Egypt, managing Tibco Spotfire and cloud products on AWS and Azure, building a team of 15, coordinating deployments, and developing AWS automation scripts.

From April 2011 to April 2013, Ibrahim Abdel Aziz was an Administrator, Automation & Monitoring Engineer at Synechron Technologies Limited in Giza, Egypt, managing Linux servers, executing patching, and automating tasks with shell scripting.

From January 2009 to March 2011, Ibrahim Abdel Aziz was a Developer, Automation & Monitoring Engineer at Tsys Limited in Cairo, Egypt, managing user access, administering databases, and developing automation scripts.

Egypt is a land steeped in ancient history, yet pulsing with modern ambition, a backdrop against which a skilled technologist like Ibrahim Abdel Aziz has built his career. From the Mediterranean port city of Alexandria, where he began as a Senior System Engineer supporting critical imaging solutions for Genworth, to the bustling capital of Cairo, Egypt has consistently provided the stage for his professional growth. He first honed his skills at Patni Computer Systems, troubleshooting the intricacies of the P8 FileNet application. Later, in Cairo, he moved through roles at Tsys Limited and Synechron Technologies, mastering server administration, database management, and the art of automation through shell scripting – a digital craftsman shaping efficiency within established systems. Now, as a Cloud Operations Architect at Egy-Tech Solutions, he’s not merely maintaining the infrastructure, but *building* it, leading a team of fifteen and architecting solutions on the cutting edge of AWS and Azure. He’s a key player in deploying and automating cloud products, particularly Tibco Spotfire, demonstrating a clear trajectory from problem-solver to innovator, all within the dynamic context of Egypt’s evolving technological landscape. The country itself seems to mirror his journey – a foundation of enduring strength supporting a rapidly developing future.

Shell scripts, born from the hands of a Senior System Engineer, are the quiet workhorses of system administration and cloud orchestration. Not flashy, but relentlessly efficient, these concise programs are masters of automation. They’ve diligently maintained the health of critical applications – eProcess, Trexo, bespoke FileNet instances – by tirelessly purging outdated logs from production servers, ensuring peak performance and valuable disk space isn’t wasted. 

But their reach extends far beyond on-premise servers. These scripts are equally at home in the cloud, specifically within the AWS ecosystem. They’re adept at identifying and gracefully retiring idle instances, reclaiming valuable resources and optimizing costs. Unused Elastic IP addresses don’t linger long under their watchful eye, swiftly returned to the pool for others to utilize. 

More recently, they’ve evolved, lending their logic to the serverless world through AWS Lambda functions, becoming event-driven automatons responding to triggers with precision. They aren’t merely tools; they are extensions of a proactive mindset, constantly seeking to streamline, optimize, and maintain the delicate balance of complex systems. They represent a dedication to preventative maintenance and a commitment to keeping the digital infrastructure running smoothly, silently, and reliably.

Shell scripting is a quietly powerful capability, a foundational skill honed over years of system administration and automation. It’s not flashy, but it’s the bedrock upon which streamlined operations are built. This isn’t merely knowing commands; it’s the art of weaving them together – a digital choreography to manage complex systems, particularly within the Linux/Unix world. 

From 2007 to 2013, it was a constant companion, used to tame the intricacies of Linux administration and ensure the smooth replication of MySQL databases. It wasn’t just about keeping things running, but about anticipating needs and building solutions for customized applications. Later, as an Automation & Monitoring Engineer at Synechron Technologies, Ibrahim Abdel Aziz wielded it specifically to bring order to Linux servers, automating repetitive tasks and ensuring consistent patching – a testament to its reliability. 

It’s a skill often found alongside broader expertise in cloud platforms like AWS and Azure, and deeply intertwined with the principles of DevOps. While Python often takes center stage in modern automation, shell scripting remains a vital tool, a quick and direct route to solving immediate problems and building essential infrastructure. It’s a core component of a Senior System Engineer’s toolkit, a silent workhorse enabling efficiency and control. It’s a practical, results-oriented skill, less about theoretical elegance and more about getting things *done*.

Azure is a powerful and versatile cloud platform, a digital landscape where Ibrahim Abdel Aziz has become a seasoned architect. Not a place, but a toolkit – a collection of services enabling scalable solutions, and a key component in the modern technological world. For over a decade, since May 2013, Abdel Aziz has wielded Azure’s capabilities at Egy-Tech Solutions, alongside its rival AWS, building and managing complex systems. He doesn’t simply *use* Azure; he orchestrates it, deploying solutions, automating processes with scripts honed in Python and shell, and ensuring its smooth operation. 

He’s a builder, having fostered a team of fifteen, guiding them through deployments and leveraging Azure’s potential. While comfortable across a spectrum of technologies – from the structured world of SQL and Oracle databases to the flexibility of Linux and Windows – Azure represents a core competency. It’s a platform he understands intimately, a foundation upon which he constructs robust and efficient cloud infrastructure. 

Azure isn’t merely a skill listed on a resume; it’s a practical domain where Abdel Aziz thrives, a space where his expertise in DevOps, automation, and solution architecture converge. It’s a platform valued enough that even seasoned Senior System Engineers seek to master its intricacies, recognizing its importance in the evolving technological landscape. It’s a dynamic environment, and Abdel Aziz is its architect.

MySQL is a robust and versatile relational database management system, a cornerstone in the toolkit of any skilled database administrator or developer. It’s a workhorse, capable of handling everything from straightforward data storage to complex transactional processing. Though often found powering web applications, its adaptability extends far beyond, readily integrating into diverse systems and architectures. 

This particular instance of MySQL’s relevance is highlighted by its connection to Ibrahim Abdel Aziz, a seasoned engineer with a history of hands-on administration. From his time at Tsys Limited (2009-2011), he demonstrated proficiency in its administration – managing user access and ensuring its smooth operation. He isn’t simply a user, but someone who understands its inner workings, capable of tailoring it to specific needs. 

His broader skillset – encompassing Linux/Unix environments, scripting languages like shell and Python, and cloud platforms like AWS and Azure – suggests a capacity to deploy and maintain MySQL within modern, scalable infrastructures. He’s not confined to traditional on-premise setups; he can integrate MySQL into automated, cloud-based pipelines. This is further reinforced by his experience in DevOps and automation, indicating an ability to streamline database tasks and ensure consistent performance. 

Essentially, MySQL in this context isn’t just a database; it’s a component expertly managed by a Senior System Engineer who understands its place within a larger, interconnected technological ecosystem. It’s a reliable foundation, strengthened by a capable hand.

Ibrahim Abdel Aziz is a seasoned Cloud Operations Architect, a builder of systems and teams, currently shaping the cloud infrastructure at Egy-Tech Solutions in Cairo. For over a decade, he’s been a pragmatic problem-solver, fluent in the languages of both cloud platforms – AWS and Azure – and the intricate workings of Tibco Spotfire. He doesn’t just manage technology; he orchestrates it, having grown and led a team of fifteen engineers. 

His foundation is deeply rooted in engineering, holding a Bachelor’s degree in Electronics and Telecommunications from Pune University, complemented by a Post Graduate Diploma in Information Technology from Symbiosis Institute. This blend of hardware understanding and software acumen informs his approach to automation, a skill honed over years spent scripting in shell and managing complex systems. 

Ibrahim’s career began with a focus on automation and monitoring, first as a Developer at Tsys Limited, where he secured systems with user access management and maintained databases, then as an Administrator at Synechron Technologies Limited, where he tamed Linux servers and streamlined operations through scripting. He’s a professional who consistently seeks to improve efficiency and reliability, a trait that has propelled him from foundational roles to architecting entire cloud environments. He can be reached via email at ibrahim.abdelaziz@email.com or by phone at +20 100 123 4567, and his work can be explored further on his professional profiles.

TIBCO Spotfire is a powerful analytics platform, a digital workspace where data transforms into actionable insight. It’s a tool favored by those who need to explore complex information – visualizing trends, discovering patterns, and predicting outcomes with speed and precision. More than just software, Spotfire is a catalyst for data-driven decisions, supporting both enterprise-level deployments and flexible cloud solutions on platforms like AWS and Azure. 

It thrives under the guidance of architects like Ibrahim Abdel Aziz, who, since 2013, has not only mastered its intricacies but built and led teams dedicated to its support and evolution. He’s a builder, orchestrating deployments, swiftly applying hotfixes, and automating processes with scripting languages like Python. Spotfire benefits from this kind of focused expertise, becoming more robust and responsive under his care. 

While it integrates seamlessly with established database systems – Oracle, MySQL, SQL – and operates comfortably across diverse operating systems, its true strength lies in empowering analysts and engineers to *interrogate* data, not just report on it. It’s a key component in a modern data stack, often found alongside tools like P8 FileNet, and is particularly valuable when coupled with DevOps methodologies and a solution-oriented architectural approach. Spotfire isn’t simply *used* – it’s actively shaped and refined by those who understand its potential to unlock hidden value within data.

Ibrahim Abdel Aziz is a seasoned Cloud Operations Architect, a builder and optimizer of digital infrastructure. For over a decade, since 2013, he’s been deeply immersed in the world of cloud computing, specifically wielding the power of AWS – and increasingly, Azure – to streamline operations and drive efficiency. He doesn’t just *use* the cloud; he *shapes* it, crafting bespoke automation solutions with shell scripts and event-driven Lambda functions to hunt down wasted resources – idle instances, orphaned IP addresses – and reclaim them. 

Based in Cairo with Egy-Tech Solutions, Ibrahim isn’t a solitary engineer. He’s a leader, having built and mentored a team of fifteen, guiding deployments and fostering a DevOps culture. His expertise isn’t limited to the cloud itself; he’s fluent in the languages of the server room – Linux, Unix, even IBM AIX and Windows – and comfortable navigating the complexities of databases like MySQL, Oracle, and SQL. He understands the bigger picture, applying solution architecture principles and leveraging tools like Tibco Spotfire and P8 FileNet to deliver comprehensive solutions. 

He’s a pragmatic problem-solver, a hands-on architect who understands that the true value of cloud technology lies in its ability to be automated, optimized, and ultimately, to empower businesses. His skills are sought after, a testament to his ability to bridge the gap between complex systems and tangible results – a core strength recognized even by those stepping into Senior System Engineer roles.
